description: Queries designed to produce yellow/signal divergence between search paradigms

queries.query: What happens to in-flight API requests when a user's session token expires mid-request? Trace the error handling from the 401 response back to the UI.
What would break if the WebSocket reconnection delay were reduced from 3 seconds to 0? Trace all downstream effects.
Find every place the app makes an optimistic update (updates UI before server confirms). What rollback mechanisms exist for each?
Under what conditions can a user see a message in channel A that was actually posted in channel B? Trace the message routing logic.
What is the maximum memory a single channel view can consume? Trace the data retained in Redux selectors for posts, reactions, files, and user profiles when viewing a channel with 10,000 messages.
What happens when two concurrent requests try to update the same model instance with select_for_update? Trace the locking mechanism from ORM to database backend.
What is the exact order that Django middleware processes a request that raises an exception in the view? Trace the unwinding through all middleware layers.
Find all places Django silently swallows exceptions (catches and continues without re-raising). Are any of these dangerous?
What happens to a yield-dependency's cleanup code if the endpoint raises an HTTPException? Does the generator always get closed?
Can a request-scoped dependency accidentally share state between concurrent requests? Trace the dependency cache lifecycle.
Using at most 25 tool calls, find the single most impactful performance bottleneck in the channel switching flow.
Using at most 20 tool calls, identify which Redux selectors are called most frequently and whether any are missing memoization.
Using at most 25 tool calls, find the most important security vulnerability in Django's CSRF protection implementation.
Using at most 20 tool calls, determine whether FastAPI's dependency injection is thread-safe for sync dependencies.
Using at most 15 tool calls, find the most critical accessibility (a11y) violation in the main navigation components.
